findingDetails.title: Canonical permission example over-grants before least-authority reduction
findingDetails.evidence: The canonical starter permission object allows broad capabilities while nearby guidance requires least-authority choices and the permission reference marks external_directory high-risk unless clearly needed.
findingDetails.recommendation: Make the starter block illustrative or default high-risk/default-unneeded permissions to deny/ask unless role-specific proof requires allow.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Direct reads of SKILL.md and permission_design.md; validation commands reported zero blocking issues.

findingDetails.title: Linked /create:agent workflows default OpenCode agent output to a non-runtime directory
findingDetails.evidence: The create-agent packet routes readers to the command workflow YAML as the machine-executed /create:agent path while the packet contract says OpenCode agents belong under .opencode/agents/. The linked auto and confirm workflows set runtime_agent_path_resolution.default to .opencode/agent and later consume runtime/agent path tokens for checking and writing generated agents; .opencode/agent is absent while .opencode/agents exists.
findingDetails.recommendation: Align both workflow YAML defaults and all runtime path examples to .opencode/agents/, or prove setup resolution injects the correct runtime path before YAML use.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Grep across .opencode/commands/create/assets/create_agent_*.yaml found the singular default in both auto and confirm workflows; direct target reads found .opencode/agents/ as the OpenCode/default placement contract.

findingDetails.title: Canonical permission example over-grants before least-authority reduction
findingDetails.evidence: The canonical starter permission object still allows broad capabilities while nearby guidance requires least-authority choices and the permission reference marks high-risk permissions as deny unless clearly needed.
findingDetails.recommendation: Make the starter block illustrative or default high-risk/default-unneeded permissions to deny/ask unless role-specific proof requires allow.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Re-read SKILL.md and permission_design.md during iteration 2; issue remains active.

noveltyJustification: One new P1 traceability finding adds weighted severity 5 over accumulated weighted severity 6; prior P2 remains active and no P0 was found.

focus: fabricated tool/flag/section claims in create-agent validation guidance

findingDetails.title: Linked /create:agent workflows default OpenCode agent output to a non-runtime directory
findingDetails.evidence: Rechecked in iteration 3: the auto workflow still sets runtime_agent_path_resolution.default to .opencode/agent, and the confirm workflow still sets the same singular default, while create-agent guidance uses .opencode/agents/ for OpenCode output.
findingDetails.recommendation: Align both workflow YAML defaults and all runtime path examples to .opencode/agents/, or prove setup resolution injects the correct runtime path before YAML use.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Direct reads of both create_agent_auto.yaml and create_agent_confirm.yaml in iteration 3 confirmed the prior mismatch remains active; not re-filed as a duplicate.

findingDetails.title: Validation gate promises runtime/link checks the cited validators do not implement
findingDetails.evidence: SKILL.md lists filename/name parity, runtime directory correctness, permission consistency, related-resource existence, and unresolved-link reporting as required delivery checks immediately after naming only validate_document.py and extract_structure.py; the shared validator parser only exposes documentation-format flags and extract_structure.py only accepts a filepath.
findingDetails.recommendation: Separate script-backed validation from manual required checks, or add/link an agent-specific validator for filename/frontmatter parity, runtime path, permission consistency, related-resource links, and unresolved markdown links.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Target docs cite only shared validators for this gate; README says no packet-local scripts exist; direct reads of the two cited validator parsers found no CLI or code path for the promised runtime/link/permission checks.

findingDetails.title: Canonical permission example over-grants before least-authority reduction
findingDetails.evidence: Rechecked in iteration 3: the canonical starter permission object and asset template still grant broad write/edit/bash/memory/list/external_directory permissions before role-specific reduction.
findingDetails.recommendation: Make the starter block illustrative or default high-risk/default-unneeded permissions to deny/ask unless role-specific proof requires allow.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Direct reads of SKILL.md and assets/agent_template.md confirmed the prior P2 remains active; not re-filed as a duplicate.

noveltyJustification: One new P1 was confirmed while the prior active P1 and P2 remain active; weighted new severity 5 over active weighted total 11 = 0.45.

findingDetails.title: Linked /create:agent workflows default OpenCode agent output to a non-runtime directory
findingDetails.evidence: Iteration 4 recheck: SKILL.md still declares OpenCode/default output under .opencode/agents/, while both create_agent_auto.yaml and create_agent_confirm.yaml still default runtime_agent_path_resolution to singular .opencode/agent.
findingDetails.recommendation: Align both workflow YAML defaults and all runtime path examples to .opencode/agents/, or prove setup resolution injects the correct runtime path before YAML use.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Direct target and command-workflow reads in iteration 4 confirmed the mismatch remains active; not re-filed as a duplicate.

findingDetails.title: Validation gate promises runtime/link checks the cited validators do not implement
findingDetails.evidence: Iteration 4 recheck: SKILL.md still lists runtime path, permission/authority, task intent, related-resource, and unresolved-link checks immediately after the same two shared validator commands; README still prescribes that same command pair and does not add a separate checker.
findingDetails.recommendation: Split script-backed checks from manual checks or add/link an agent-specific validator that verifies runtime path, filename/name parity, permissions, related-resource links, and unresolved markdown links.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Current target reads confirmed the overstated gate text remains; iteration 3 script reads remain the counterevidence for validator capabilities.

findingDetails.title: Canonical permission example over-grants before least-authority reduction
findingDetails.evidence: Iteration 4 recheck: SKILL.md and the asset template still grant broad starter permissions, including external_directory and default MCP examples, while permission_design.md says high-risk tools should be denied unless clearly needed and every allow must map to role text.
findingDetails.recommendation: Make starter blocks explicitly illustrative or default high-risk/default-unneeded permissions and MCP servers to deny/ask/empty unless role-specific proof requires allow.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Direct reads of SKILL.md, permission_design.md, and agent_template.md in iteration 4 confirmed the prior advisory remains active; command workflow defaults were treated as same-class supporting evidence rather than a duplicate finding.

noveltyJustification: Final required security iteration found no new active findings; all three known findings were rechecked and classified active without duplicate filing.
